front: Play: phonetics professor bets he can pass a Cockney flower girl off as a duchess; she exclaims 'Not bloody likely!'
Play: the essay 'What Happened Afterwards' explains the flower girl married Freddy, not the professor, and opened a flower shop
Play: Jack Tanner marries Ann Whitefield who embodies the 'Life Force'; third act 'Don Juan in Hell' set in the Sierra Nevada
Play: the Undershaft family of munitions manufacturers only bequeaths wealth to foundlings; Adolphus Cusins must reveal his foundling status
Play: about Joan of Arc's canonization; she identifies the dauphin on sight and changes the wind; called a 'tragedy without villains'
Play: Vivie discovers her mother manages brothels; one of the 'Plays Unpleasant'
Play: Captain Bluntschli puts chocolate in his pockets instead of ammunition during the Serbo-Bulgarian War
back: Arms and the Man (George Bernard Shaw)

front: Playwright: coined 'Bardolatry'; will funded a phonetic alphabet in which only Androcles and the Lion was published
